Pioneering yellow home companion robot (2003) from Mitsubishi Heavy Industries; ~1 m tall on a wheeled base with two arms, face/voice recognition and a 10,000-word vocabulary for household assistance.
NEC's PaPeRo (Partner-type Personal Robot), introduced in 2001: a 38 cm home companion with face and speech recognition, a long-running icon of Japanese personal robotics.
MIT's late-1990s expressive robotic head by Cynthia Breazeal; a pioneer of social robotics and affective computing that recognised and simulated emotions.
Released in 2017, Jibo was billed as the first social robot for the home: a 28 cm swivelling tabletop companion with a round animated face, by Cynthia Breazeal's startup.
Expressive social robot with an 8-inch screen face, deployed in classrooms and hospitals to assist sick children and elderly patients via interactive telepresence.
AI educational companion robot that teaches language and STEM subjects to early learners using interactive voice games and facial emotion detection.
Highly expressive, adorable domestic companion robot with a 1080p camera behind its eyes, capacitive touch sensors, and a friendly personality, retired in 2018.
The modern generation of Sony's robotic dog, using cloud-based AI, SLAM mapping, and capacitive touch sensors to develop a unique personality over time.
Third-generation emotional care robot with an upgraded NVIDIA Jetson brain, 50+ sensors, a thermography camera, and multi-layered OLED eyes for lifelike warmth.
